title: What I think about over training

transcript.text: Hello, this video is what I think about overtraining.
I have been sick, and it's strange because I'm never sick.
And I was really sick for like a week, just a couple, why am I getting sick again?
Diet, what is the deal?
And I think it's because I'm exercising.
And you know, my body's just not used to exercising long without exercising.
So I'm thinking, if you feel, well I don't really feel sick.
I feel like, I'm not sore, but if I try to work out, I'm obviously a lot weaker than normal.
And you know, my legs, my muscles, all of my muscles will just burn a lot quicker and stuff.
So, you know, like, what does that mean?
What should I do?
Should I keep working out, or should I rest?
I've asked a couple trainers at my gym.
This dude, Chang, he's like, oh yeah, it takes 72 hours to recover from muscle burn.
And I'm like, well, should you work out when you're sore?
And he's like, oh yeah, why not?
And then this other guy's like, well, why don't you rest?
And so, anyways, my idea is, I think it's good to train even when you're sore and you're at diminished strength,
As long as it doesn't get you sick, as long as it doesn't give you a full-fledged fever.
And I'm not at a full-fledged fever, so I'll keep on working out.
I just won't totally bust my ass, you know.
But I have my own methods of training.
I watch the chicks on most DIYmuscle.com and do their workout, and they're always, you know, really weak,
So I can do that shit pretty easily.
But, well, you know, most people, they just don't have the time to work out.
So if you're overtraining, then don't work out, obviously.
But if you have nothing better to do, you know, I don't.
Oh, yeah, moral of the story, I don't think overtraining is actually something that exists.
One person who can tell you if you're going to get sick, and that's you.
If tomorrow I'm sitting around, my work gets tied up because my computer gets tied up,
Then I'll work out, even if I'm weak.
That's how it goes.
There's no such thing as work is overtraining, only if it makes you sick.
Am I right?
I don't know.
